ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your social media use is within a healthy range and does not appear to interfere with your daily life or well-being. You demonstrate good self-regulation over your online activity and maintain a balanced relationship with social media platforms.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your moderate and balanced social media usage habits
Periodically check your screen time statistics to stay aware of any gradual increases
Be mindful of how social media content affects your mood and self-image

ranges.label: Mild Risk
ranges.description: You show some signs of unhealthy social media usage patterns, such as spending more time than intended or feeling restless without access. While not yet significantly disruptive, these early warning signs suggest your relationship with social media may benefit from proactive adjustments.
suggestions: Set specific daily time limits for social media use using built-in screen time tools
Turn off non-essential push notifications to reduce habitual checking
Develop offline social activities and hobbies to diversify how you spend your leisure time
Establish phone-free periods during meals and before bedtime

ranges.label: Moderate Risk
ranges.description: Your social media use is likely affecting your daily life, interpersonal relationships, and emotional well-being. You may find it difficult to stop scrolling, feel anxious when unable to check your accounts, or notice that social media is replacing meaningful offline activities.
suggestions: Try a structured digital detox by designating full days or weekends without social media
Use app-specific screen time management tools to enforce daily usage limits
Reflect on how social media affects your mood, sleep quality, and self-esteem
Actively seek alternative leisure activities such as exercise, reading, or face-to-face socializing
If you feel distressed or unable to cut back on your own, consider consulting a mental health professional

ranges.label: High Risk
ranges.description: Your social media usage pattern has reached a problematic level consistent with behavioral addiction criteria. It is likely seriously affecting your daily functioning, work or academic performance, sleep, and relationships. Compulsive use despite negative consequences is a hallmark of this stage.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to consult a mental health professional experienced in behavioral addictions
Create a detailed, written usage restriction plan with specific daily limits and accountability measures
Consider temporarily uninstalling your most-used social media apps to break the cycle
Ask trusted family members or friends to support and monitor your reduction efforts
Address any underlying emotional issues such as loneliness, anxiety, or low self-esteem that may drive compulsive use

ranges.label: Non-Problem Gambling
ranges.description: You did not report any signs of problem gambling. Your responses suggest that gambling, if present at all, is recreational and well-controlled. You do not appear to be at risk for gambling-related harm at this time.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy recreational habits and responsible attitudes toward gambling
If you do gamble, always set firm time and money limits before you begin
Stay informed about the warning signs of problem gambling so you can recognize changes early

ranges.label: Low Risk Gambling
ranges.description: You reported minor gambling-related concerns at a low risk level. While these issues are not currently severe, they indicate that gambling is beginning to have a small but noticeable presence in your life. Maintaining awareness now can help prevent escalation.
suggestions: Maintain honest self-awareness about your gambling behavior and its effects on your life
Set clear and strict limits on both gambling time and money before each session
Never use gambling as a way to cope with stress, boredom, or emotional difficulties
Track your gambling expenditures over time to identify any upward trends

ranges.label: Moderate Risk Gambling
ranges.description: Your gambling behavior is causing some negative consequences and may be moving beyond recreational activity. You may be spending more money or time than intended, chasing losses, or experiencing guilt or relationship strain related to gambling.
suggestions: Seriously examine how gambling is affecting your finances, relationships, and emotional health
Set strict gambling budgets and stick to them without exception, never chasing losses
Consider calling a gambling helpline for confidential advice and support
Learn about cognitive distortions common in gambling, such as the illusion of control and the gambler's fallacy
Explore healthier alternative activities to fill the time and excitement that gambling currently provides

ranges.label: Problem Gambling
ranges.description: Your gambling behavior has caused significant negative consequences across multiple areas of your life, meeting criteria for problem gambling. You may be experiencing financial hardship, damaged relationships, emotional distress, or loss of control over your gambling behavior.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek professional gambling treatment from a qualified counselor or therapist
Let trusted family members or friends know about your situation so they can provide support
Consider enrolling in a self-exclusion program to ban yourself from gambling venues and online platforms
Address any underlying emotional or mental health issues such as depression or anxiety that may fuel gambling
Join a peer support group such as Gamblers Anonymous to connect with others in recovery

ranges.label: No Alcohol Problems Indicated
ranges.description: Your responses do not suggest the presence of alcohol-related problems. You appear to have a healthy relationship with alcohol, whether through moderate consumption or abstinence. No further clinical evaluation is indicated at this time.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy drinking habits or abstinence from alcohol
Stay informed about the recommended alcohol intake guidelines and the risks of excessive drinking
Be mindful of any future changes in your drinking patterns and reassess periodically

ranges.label: Possible Alcohol Problem
ranges.description: Your response suggests a possible alcohol-related concern that warrants attention. While a single positive answer does not confirm an alcohol use disorder, it may indicate early signs of problematic drinking patterns. Further self-reflection or professional evaluation could be beneficial.
suggestions: Reflect honestly on your relationship with alcohol and whether it has changed over time
Consider discussing your drinking habits with a healthcare provider at your next visit
Monitor your drinking patterns closely over the coming weeks using a simple diary or tracking app
Learn about recommended alcohol intake guidelines to establish a personal benchmark

ranges.label: Probable Alcohol Problem
ranges.description: A score of 2 or more on the CAGE is clinically significant and indicates a probable alcohol use problem. Your responses suggest patterns consistent with alcohol dependence or harmful use, such as feeling the need to cut down, experiencing guilt, or using alcohol to manage withdrawal symptoms.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek a professional evaluation for alcohol use from a healthcare provider or addiction specialist
Consult with your primary care physician, who can assess your situation and recommend appropriate treatment options
Consider joining a support group such as Alcoholics Anonymous for peer support and structured recovery
Talk openly with trusted family members or friends who can provide emotional support during this process
